title: [GVN] Support rnflow pattern matching and transform
author: madhur13490
description:
This is an alternative patch to #144987, adding support in GVN.
In this patch, I added support in GVN to recognize PRE load and hoist it out of the loop. This patch leads to ~15% improvement for the rnflow benchmark in Polyhedron testsuite when tested on NVIDIA's Grace platform.

Testing:
1. Various internal workloads and benchmarks
2. LLVM Test-suite
3. LLVM regressions tests.

No crash, all tests pass.
In this revision, I am keeping the feature off-by-default and the following patch will just flip the switch.

Assisted by Cursor

tests.test_body:
define void @test_gvn_min_pattern_i32(ptr %arr, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.i32.iv = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load i32, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.i32.min = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load i32, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_min_pattern(ptr %0, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.float.iv = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load float, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.float.min = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load float, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp contract olt float %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_min_pattern_i64(ptr %0, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.i64.iv = getelementptr i64, ptr %0, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i64.iv, i64 -16
  %val.first = load i64, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 8
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.i64.min = getelementptr i64, ptr %0, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i64.min, i64 -8
  %val.current.min = load i64, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 8
  %cmp = icmp slt i64 %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_condition_not_cmp(ptr %arr, i32 %n, i1 %bool) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %i to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %sel = select i1 %bool, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define i32 @test_gvn_min_pattern_offset_mismatch(ptr %base) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %iv = phi i64 [ 3, %entry ], [ %iv.next, %loop ]
  %ctr = phi i64 [ 3, %entry ], [ %ctr.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %iv.next = add nsw i64 %iv, -1
  %ptr.iv = getelementptr float, ptr %base, i64 %iv
  %val.first = load float, ptr %ptr.iv, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %gep.typed = getelementptr float, ptr %base, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %gep.offset = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep.typed, i64 -4
  %val.min = load float, ptr %gep.offset, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %val.first, %val.min
  %new.idx = trunc nsw i64 %iv.next to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %new.idx, i32 %min.idx
  %ctr.next = add nsw i64 %ctr, -1
  %cond = icmp samesign ugt i64 %ctr, 1
  br i1 %cond, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret i32 %min.idx.next
}

define void @test_wrong_gep_pattern(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep2, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_not_both_loads(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, 0.000000e+00
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float 0.000000e+00
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_no_sext(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %zext = zext i32 %i to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %zext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_initial_index_not_one(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %i, i32 %min.idx
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_no_preheader(ptr %arr, i32 %n, i1 %cond) {
entry:
  br i1 %cond, label %loop, label %other_entry

other_entry:                                      ; preds = %entry
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %other_entry, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ 0, %other_entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %i to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_index_is_constant(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 5 to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_wrong_sext_types(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %i16 = trunc i32 %i to i16
  %sext = sext i16 %i16 to i32
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i32 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

declare void @external_function(ptr)

define void @test_may_alias_call_in_loop(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  call void @external_function(ptr %arr)
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %i, i32 %min.idx
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_non_invariant_base_ptr(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %base.ptr = phi ptr [ %arr, %entry ], [ %base.ptr.next, %loop ]
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %base.ptr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %base.ptr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %i, i32 %min.idx
  %base.ptr.next = getelementptr float, ptr %base.ptr, i32 1
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_not_in_loop(ptr %arr) {
entry:
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 0
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 1 to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  ret void
}

define void @test_aliasing_store_in_loop(ptr %arr, i32 %n) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %i = phi i32 [ 0, %entry ], [ %i.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ 1, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %store.gep = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  store float 0.000000e+00, ptr %store.gep, align 4
  %gep1 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i32 %i
  %load1 = load float, ptr %gep1, align 4
  %sext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %gep2 = getelementptr float, ptr %arr, i64 %sext
  %gep3 = getelementptr i8, ptr %gep2, i64 0
  %load2 = load float, ptr %gep3, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp olt float %load1, %load2
  %sel = select i1 %cmp, float %load1, float %load2
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %i, i32 %min.idx
  %i.next = add i32 %i, 1
  %exit = icmp slt i32 %i.next, %n
  br i1 %exit, label %loop, label %exit_block

exit_block:                                       ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_pattern_icmp_eq(ptr %arr, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.i32.iv = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load i32, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.i32.min = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load i32, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_pattern_icmp_ult(ptr %arr, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.i32.iv = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load i32, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.i32.min = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load i32, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = icmp ult i32 %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_pattern_matched_load_on_lhs(ptr %0, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.float.iv = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load float, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.float.min = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load float, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp contract ogt float %val.current.min, %val.first
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %min.idx, i32 %next.idx.trunc
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_pattern_gt_predicate(ptr %0, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.float.iv = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load float, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.float.min = getelementptr float, ptr %0, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.float.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load float, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = fcmp contract ogt float %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

define void @test_gvn_pattern_icmp_sgt(ptr %arr, i32 %initial_min_idx) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:                                             ; preds = %loop, %entry
  %indvars.iv.i = phi i64 [ 1, %entry ], [ %indvars.iv.next.i, %loop ]
  %loop.counter = phi i64 [ 0, %entry ], [ %loop.counter.next, %loop ]
  %min.idx = phi i32 [ %initial_min_idx, %entry ], [ %min.idx.next, %loop ]
  %indvars.iv.next.i = add nsw i64 %indvars.iv.i, -1
  %ptr.i32.iv = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %indvars.iv.i
  %ptr.first.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.iv, i64 -8
  %val.first = load i32, ptr %ptr.first.load, align 4
  %min.idx.ext = sext i32 %min.idx to i64
  %ptr.i32.min = getelementptr i32, ptr %arr, i64 %min.idx.ext
  %ptr.second.load = getelementptr i8, ptr %ptr.i32.min, i64 -4
  %val.current.min = load i32, ptr %ptr.second.load, align 4
  %cmp = icmp sgt i32 %val.first, %val.current.min
  %next.idx.trunc = trunc nsw i64 %indvars.iv.next.i to i32
  %min.idx.next = select i1 %cmp, i32 %next.idx.trunc, i32 %min.idx
  %loop.counter.next = add nsw i64 %loop.counter, -1
  %loop.continue = icmp samesign ugt i64 %loop.counter, 1
  br i1 %loop.continue, label %loop, label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %loop
  ret void
}

comments.author: madhur13490
comments.body:
Hi @fhahn  and @nikic,
This patch requires some cosmetic changes. My concern is this is to understand if this what GVN should do?

The patch is functionally correct as the rnflow application passes. However, compared to trunk this patch **regresses** the app by ~10-12% as the new select instruction the pass is generating is more constly than hoisting the load. I played around `SelectOptimize.cpp` by turning on --disable-loop-level-heuristics. That helps a bit but still ~10% regression persists.

comments.author: david-arm
comments.body:
> The patch is functionally correct as the rnflow application passes. However, compared to trunk this patch regresses the app by ~10-12% as the new select instruction the pass is generating is more constly than hoisting the load. I played around SelectOptimize.cpp by turning on --disable-loop-level-heuristics. That helps a bit but still ~10% regression persists.

In the test you've added the original IR seems to already have a select instruction - do you also see a select in rnflow prior to GVN?

comments.author: david-arm
comments.body: Sorry, please ignore me! I see there is an extra select being generated. Got it. :)

> The patch is functionally correct as the rnflow application passes. However, compared to trunk this patch **regresses** the app by ~10-12% as the new select instruction the pass is generating is more constly than hoisting the load. I feel like I saw a comment somewhere that doing the GVN transform would improve performance slightly, but I have trouble finding it, maybe I imagined it...

comments.author: madhur13490
comments.body:
Hi @nikic and @david-arm 
With the latest rebase, I do see a performance gain with this patch contrary to the earlier reported regression.

Either this due to recent patches in LLVM or refreshed setup at my end. It is complicated to pinpoint to the patch which might have helped this patch gain the perf. Nevertheless, as it stands today, I see 15% end to end perf gain in Polyhedron's rnflow app on AArch64 Grace platform. My colleague, @ssijaric-nv  also checked it on X86, AMD EPYC Milan1 and he sees slightly less gain but indeed there is roughtly 6% gain. 

I have cleaned up this patch and is ready for review. Can you please have a look?

Hi @nikic,
Thanks for the review.  I have addressed most of them and replied with "Done"

>Why do we care about the predicate here at all? Shouldn't this transform be valid for any predicate? Maybe it's a max instead of min, but that shouldn't really matter, right?

Ideally, yes, you're right but I am being extra careful and would like to handle max case in a separate patch. The followup patch will generalize this and add tests for max case too.

>You can use m_PtrAdd to ensure i8 type for the OffsetVal GEP. For the other one you'd need to explicitly capture it as a GEPOperator/GetElementPtrInst and inspect the type.
Done.


Can you please resolve the comments as you feel happy about it? This will allow me to focus on the unresolved ones.

@alinas Thanks for taking time and reviewing the code. I have addressed your review comments and also

comments.author: madhur13490
comments.body:
>Could you also please test this on the llvm test suite? This is the kind of change that has a high chance of introducing a hard to track down miscompile, unless tested well beforehand.
Also see above about having it under a flag at first, for similar reasons.

I tested llvm test-suite with the recent version, I don't see any crash or abnormal behaviour. 

About the flag: 
I don't mind having cl::opt flag but I prefer it to be on by default. I see your point about keeping the feature off by default, especially from a safety perspective. That said, having it off introduces an extra path that is rarely exercised and adds downstream operational overhead for us. I’d prefer to keep it on by default and rely on our existing monitoring/rollback mechanisms, so the behaviour we have is the one we actually test and maintain. 

I have tested this patch widely on various benchmarks and workloads and since the pattern is quite specific, I believe the miscompile is rare. 

IIUC, are you suggesting that we land this commit with off by default and then in the successive commit we just flip the switch to make the revert easy, in case of miscompiles?

comments.author: alinas
comments.body:
> IIUC, are you suggesting that we land this commit with off by default and then in the successive commit we just flip the switch to make the revert easy, in case of miscompiles?

Yes, precisely. If an issue is found, rather than reverting all changes in this PR, you can then revert the flag flip, fix the issue, then re-enable the flag.
Adding: once the change is stable, you can remove the flag.

comments.author: momchil-velikov
comments.body:
AFAIK, there are still a couple of suggestions:
 * no being dependent on the specific condition - the transform cares that we choose one or another index, but not about the specific criteria involved in choosing it, right?
 * what if the indices are `i32` or `i64` throughout with no conversion shenanigans?

comments.author: madhur13490
comments.body:
> AFAIK, there are still a couple of suggestions:
> 
> * no being dependent on the specific condition - the transform cares that we choose one or another index, but not about the specific criteria involved in choosing it, right?

Thanks @momchil for reminding me.  I have updated the patch to 
* made it more generic and that should handle your's and @nikic comments. Summarized the predicate generalisation (filter removed; LHS-matched case canonicalized via swapOperands()
* covered by the two new positive tests

For second suggestion,
> * what if the indices are `i32` or `i64` throughout with no conversion shenanigans?

I would like make that further generalization as a separate patch as the transform is already a big piece. Keeping this patch simple and make the next one incremental will make the work manageable.

Ping to @nikic and @alinas to also take a look.
